markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ confirmation of a November 19, 2026 release for Grand Theft Auto VI has extended the resolution window for this market by roughly five months following earlier delays. This timeframe now captures the holiday 2026 release cycle, when major music drops, streaming milestones, AI announcements, and chart movements often accelerate. Trader sentiment centers on high-profile projects with confirmed or expected timelines—such as album rollouts and platform updates—whose outcomes can be verified well before the game ships. The extended runway heightens focus on seasonal promotional windows and any late-breaking industry news that could shift probabilities in the coming weeks.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York sex-crimes retrial, including the May 2026 deadlock on the Jessica Mann rape charge, have solidified trader consensus around the No Prison Time outcome. The 2024 overturn of his original New York conviction and the mixed 2025 retrial verdict limited new exposure, while ongoing appeals of the consecutive 16-year California sentence—still under review by skeptical appellate panels—introduce further uncertainty. Weinstein’s documented health challenges and time already served at Rikers reinforce the market’s view that additional significant incarceration is unlikely absent successful new prosecutions. Upcoming decisions on whether prosecutors pursue another retrial or appeal outcomes will serve as key catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights shapes current trader consensus, with the Hurricanes as the implied favorite entering Game 1 on home ice. Carolina arrives with a dominant 12-1 playoff mark after closing out the Montreal Canadiens in five games during the Eastern Conference Final, showcasing elite defensive structure and depth. Vegas reached the series by advancing past the Colorado Avalanche in the Western Conference Final, bolstered by prior Stanley Cup experience across its roster. These factors, combined with Carolina’s stronger recent form and home-ice advantage in a best-of-seven format, underpin the market’s positioning ahead of the series opener.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The San Antonio Spurs hold a clear edge in the 2026 NBA Finals matchup against the New York Knicks, driven by their recent Game 7 victory over the defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder that secured the Western Conference title. Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs' depth have propelled them through a strong postseason run, reflected in trader consensus around 64 percent implied probability. The Knicks, appearing in their first Finals since 1999 after an 11-game winning streak through the East, benefit from rest and momentum but trail at roughly 36 percent. Both teams' paths highlight competitive balance, with health, home-court factors, and series adjustments likely influencing outcomes in this closely contested championship.

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering among France, Spain, England, Portugal, Argentina, and Brazil reflects a wide-open field where multiple sides enter with comparable depth, recent success, and strong qualification records. Spain’s status as reigning European champions and consistent results since 2024 underpin their narrow edge, while France’s squad versatility and three prior final appearances keep them alongside despite minor injury concerns. England’s perfect qualifying campaign and Argentina’s back-to-back ambitions add further balance, with Brazil’s traditional talent pool maintaining their position. The expanded 48-team format, recent squad announcements, and minimal major disruptions in the final buildup have prevented any single nation from separating, leaving trader consensus reflecting the evenly matched top tier heading into the June kickoff.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering among France, Spain, England, Portugal, Argentina, and Brazil reflects a wide-open field where multiple sides enter with comparable depth, recent success, and strong qualification records. Spain’s status as reigning European champions and consistent results since 2024 underpin their narrow edge, while France’s squad versatility and three prior final appearances keep them alongside despite minor injury concerns. England’s perfect qualifying campaign and Argentina’s back-to-back ambitions add further balance, with Brazil’s traditional talent pool maintaining their position. The expanded 48-team format, recent squad announcements, and minimal major disruptions in the final buildup have prevented any single nation from separating, leaving trader consensus reflecting the evenly matched top tier heading into the June kickoff.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s consolidated authority within the Chinese Communist Party, backed by loyal personnel appointments, military reshuffles, and anti-corruption measures, underpins the 93.3% trader consensus that he will remain in power through 2026. Official actions through mid-2026, including his New Year address introducing the 15th Five-Year Plan and a May summit with U.S. counterparts, reflect ongoing command over policy and personnel. Analysts highlight the absence of visible successors or elite fractures ahead of the 21st Party Congress in late 2027, where a fourth term remains the baseline expectation following the prior abolition of presidential term limits. No major health events or internal challenges have emerged to alter this trajectory, though unforeseen developments could still shift outcomes before the market’s December 31, 2026 resolution.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2024 election defeat left the Democratic field wide open for 2028, with no incumbent advantage and early trader consensus reflected in current odds showing Gavin Newsom at 25.1% ahead of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 10.5% and Kamala Harris at 8.3%. Name recognition from statewide executive roles and recent national campaigns gives frontrunners an initial edge, while ideological positioning—progressive priorities versus moderate governing records—differentiates contenders and could consolidate support through primary voter turnout in early states. Fundraising capacity, Senate or gubernatorial performance, and potential endorsements from party figures or donors remain key variables that might narrow the field before formal announcements accelerate. Historical patterns of post-defeat party rebuilding suggest volatility until polling averages and convention dynamics emerge closer to the cycle.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2024 election defeat left the Democratic field wide open for 2028, with no incumbent advantage and early trader consensus reflected in current odds showing Gavin Newsom at 25.1% ahead of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 10.5% and Kamala Harris at 8.3%. Name recognition from statewide executive roles and recent national campaigns gives frontrunners an initial edge, while ideological positioning—progressive priorities versus moderate governing records—differentiates contenders and could consolidate support through primary voter turnout in early states. Fundraising capacity, Senate or gubernatorial performance, and potential endorsements from party figures or donors remain key variables that might narrow the field before formal announcements accelerate. Historical patterns of post-defeat party rebuilding suggest volatility until polling averages and convention dynamics emerge closer to the cycle.
